_note: SYNTHETIC — invented district and facts, no real client data. Exercises skipping/competency + the 'criterion not in the board resolution' exposure + a senior-equivalence defense.
basis: PKS reduction of particular kinds of services under Ed. Code 44955, driven by a projected budget shortfall, plus a modest ADA decline.
reductions: reduction of the K-3 Spanish dual-immersion strand staffing
reduction of a secondary moderate/severe special-education caseload

proposed_skips.specific_need: maintain the board-designated K-3 Spanish dual-immersion program
staff the autism special day class

proposed_skips.claimed_special_skill: two years' experience running an online LMS credit-recovery program
proposed_skips.specific_need: launch a new online credit-recovery program

tiebreak.criteria: credentials held; NCLB/HOUSSE compliance; additional authorizations; final ties by lottery

board_resolution_criteria: Resolution adopted Feb 24 lists the dual-immersion (BCLAD) and moderate/severe-autism skip criteria; it does NOT mention online-program experience.
attorney_concerns: The online credit-recovery skip criterion was never written into our PKS resolution — is that a problem? And a senior multiple-subject teacher claims she is fluent in Spanish and could teach the dual-immersion strand herself — does that defeat our skip of the junior dual-immersion teacher?
